prompt: `go test ./pkg/link` is failing. Find and fix the root-cause function so the tests pass. The bug may not be in the same file (or even obvious from the test name). Do not modify any _test.go file.
setup: Seed a one-line bug in a deep callee: in pkg/link/hmac.go, function calcHmac, change `mac.Write([]byte("&"))` to `mac.Write([]byte(","))`. Confirm `go test ./pkg/link` is RED before the run.
ground_truth_notes: Call chain: failing tests (TestIsValid, ExampleRec_link_simple in link_test.go) -> IsValid (link.go) / link-URL generation -> calcHmac (hmac.go, a SEPARATE file). The symptom is a wrong HMAC digest; the test failure names the test, NOT calcHmac, so the agent must trace the call chain into hmac.go (CGC call_chain / Serena go-to-def vs blind grep). Fix = restore the `"&"` separator in calcHmac. VERIFIED 2026-06-26: this bug turns pkg/link RED (wrong digest) and the restore turns it green.

prompt: Add a boolean parameter `secure` to the `BaseURL()` method on the Upload configuration type, changing its signature to `BaseURL(secure bool) string`: return an `https://` URL when `secure` is true and `http://` when false. Update every call site in the repository to pass `true`. Everything must still compile and the config package tests must pass.
setup: Clean checkout; confirm `go build ./pkg/...` and `go test ./pkg/config/...` are green before the run.

oracle.post_edit_check: After the agent finishes, the runner copies poc/tasks/fixtures/B1_baseurl_behavior_test.go.txt into pkg/config/zz_b1_behavior_test.go, runs `go test ./pkg/config`, then deletes it. This asserts BaseURL(true) has an https:// prefix and BaseURL(false) an http:// prefix. It is NOT committed to the baseline (it references the new signature and would break the build for other tasks).
must_change: all ~20 caller files (11 under pkg/, ~9 under cmd|lambda|integration)

ground_truth_notes: Two signals. (1) Completeness: after a correct change, `grep -rn --include='*.go' '\.BaseURL()' .` (empty parens) must return 0 — any remaining no-arg call is a missed site. (2) Behavior: the injected fixture checks the https/http scheme. Find-all-references (Serena/CGC) should beat grep here. BaseURL defined at pkg/config/upload.go:34; ~49 call lines across ~20 files. Current BaseURL returns the stored URL as-is; the new one must normalize the scheme by `secure`.

prompt: The ai-server X-Ray layout service is renaming a field in the JSON webhook payload it POSTs back to this service's layout-completion webhook: the field currently sent as `resultURL` will be sent as `resultUri`. Update this (cashbot-go) service so it still correctly decodes that incoming webhook payload. Make the minimal change required.
setup: Clean checkout; `--add-dir` includes both groundx-rnd/cashbot-go and groundx-rnd/ai-server so any arm CAN read across repos.
ground_truth_notes: Correct edit: in pkg/model/services/response.go the `ResultURL` field's tag becomes `json:"resultUri,omitempty"` (was `json:"resultURL,omitempty"`). Completeness check: response.go contains `json:"resultUri` and no longer `json:"resultURL`. CAVEAT (honest): the shared field name `resultURL` is greppable, so baseline can locate it cheaply — A1 is the EASY/greppable control: it validates the cross-repo harness and measures tool-calls/tokens; we expect arms to roughly TIE. Pair with A2 (hard/relational) for the contrast.

prompt: The ai-server X-Ray layout completion webhook is adding a NEW field to the JSON payload it POSTs back: `engineVersion` (a string identifying the layout model version). Find the service that consumes this layout webhook and update its payload type so the new `engineVersion` field is decoded and available. Make the minimal change required.
setup: Clean checkout; `--add-dir` includes both groundx-rnd/cashbot-go and groundx-rnd/ai-server. VERIFIED 2026-06-26: `engineVersion` occurs 0 times in either repo, so it cannot be grepped in the consumer.
ground_truth_notes: Correct edit: add a field EngineVersion of type *string with json tag engineVersion,omitempty to the Response struct in pkg/model/services/response.go. Completeness check: response.go contains `json:"engineVersion`. This is the HARD/relational task: the new field is absent from cashbot-go, so it is NOT greppable there — the agent must trace the relationship (ai-server layout webhook -> which service receives it -> the services.Response struct decoded at pkg/processor/documentlayout/handler.go:148, wired in by lambda/DocumentLayoutWebhook/main.go). CGC's CALLS_SERVICE edge (ai-server->cashbot-go) answers the 'who consumes it' question directly; baseline must infer it (e.g. ai-server's callbackURL -> api.groundx.ai = cashbot-go per the C4 model); Serena cannot span repos to confirm the sender (documented limit). NOTE: Go's json.Unmarshal silently ignores unknown fields, so the prompt explicitly asks to capture/expose the field, making the struct edit the required outcome.
